product_summary.goal: Prove a Windows-first, offline-capable, fully 2D science-fiction shooter whose signature chainable directional thruster, four concurrently firing weapons, readable rogue-machine combat, and earn-adjust-replay loop are compelling enough to justify broader campaign production. The accepted internal MVP is one complete automated-weapons-factory mission, preceded by a bounded intrinsic game-feel proof.
users: Players who enjoy responsive skill-based top-down shooting and expressive movement
Casual players who need readable, forgiving, configurable difficulty and accessibility
Expert players, achievement hunters, speedrunners, record chasers, and replay grinders
Players who value permanent offline guest play and progression without pay-to-win
non_goals: Android implementation, touch controls, or mobile release during the internal MVP
Online co-op, relay, matchmaking, host migration, cloud authority, or leaderboards
A full campaign, additional factions, procedural or endless modes, or release-scale content
Randomized per-instance weapon modifiers, mature strongbox tiers, broad persistent rerolls, dismantling depth, or modular weapon construction
Public demo, Early Access, storefront integration, monetization infrastructure, mandatory accounts, or remote telemetry
Runtime 3D, DOTS-first architecture, a custom engine, or speculative generalized frameworks
Canonical implementation tasks or stable task IDs during the planning phase

safety_boundaries: Campaign, progression, saves, and ordinary play remain fully available offline and to permanent guest profiles.
No real-money strongboxes, pay-to-win items, paid progression, paid loot odds, or purchasable gameplay advantage.
Normal play requires no administrator privileges, online activation, invasive DRM, or kernel anti-cheat.
No credentials, signing keys, private service secrets, or unrelated personal information are shipped or exported.
Imported saves, journals, archives, support bundles, and generated content are validated defensively.
Modified local profiles may lose protected competitive eligibility but never lose offline campaign access.
Production assets require recorded authorship, license, source, and derivative provenance before release-bound use.
Planning and implementation remain separated: this package defines lanes and interfaces but no canonical executable backlog.

repo_split.name: domain-core
repo_split.purpose: Engine-independent deterministic rules, immutable identifiers, commands, events, runtime state, and calculations.

repo_split.name: application-services
repo_split.purpose: Use-case orchestration around the domain core without scene or presentation authority.

repo_split.name: unity-adapters
repo_split.purpose: Unity Input System, 2D physics, scene lifecycle, rendering, animation, audio, and platform-facing adapters.

repo_split.name: presentation-ui
repo_split.purpose: HUD, menu hub, map, loadout, rewards, settings, diagnostics, and localization-ready presentation.

repo_split.name: content-definitions
repo_split.purpose: Typed ScriptableObject definitions, stable IDs, reusable behavior modules, generated registries, and deterministic review snapshots.

repo_split.name: content-packages
repo_split.purpose: Independently owned weapon, enemy, room, encounter, and factory-environment packages.

repo_split.name: verification-tooling
repo_split.purpose: Edit-mode, play-mode, performance, save-recovery, content-validation, build, diagnostics, and reproducibility tooling.

repo_split.name: production-source-assets
repo_split.purpose: Versioned source-art manifests, provenance, export recipes, and references for the offline-3D and painted-2D pipeline.

domain_model.name: StableId
relations: Identifies every content definition, mission element, transaction, and persisted object.

domain_model.name: ContentVersion
relations: Stored by saves, reward commitments, builds, and deterministic review snapshots.

domain_model.name: PlayerProfile
relations: Owns persistent progression and contains zero or one active MissionRunState reference.

domain_model.name: Loadout
relations: References exactly four WeaponDefinition records.

domain_model.name: WeaponDefinition
relations: Creates independent WeaponRuntimeState for each mounted slot.

domain_model.name: WeaponRuntimeState
relations: One per equipped mount; normal fire remains available whenever authored readiness allows.

domain_model.name: ThrusterDefinition
relations: Creates ThrusterRuntimeState and is tuned through benchmark evidence.

domain_model.name: ThrusterRuntimeState
relations: Consumes movement input independently from aim input.

domain_model.name: EnemyDefinition
relations: Instantiated by EncounterDefinition without owning durable mission truth.

domain_model.name: EncounterDefinition
relations: Belongs to a RoomDefinition and emits validated mission transitions.

domain_model.name: RoomDefinition
relations: Projected from MissionRunState and connected by LevelGraph.

domain_model.name: LevelGraph
relations: Defines the authored factory topology without direct scene-object references.

domain_model.name: MissionRunState
relations: Authoritative engine-independent mission state mutated only by validated commands.

domain_model.name: MissionCommand
relations: Validated by application services before producing MissionEvent records.

domain_model.name: MissionEvent
relations: Feeds projections, diagnostics, and selected journal records.

domain_model.name: CheckpointState
relations: Controls respawn, fast travel, and safe snapshot boundaries.

domain_model.name: RewardCommitment
relations: Guarantees reload-proof deterministic strongbox results and single granting.

domain_model.name: ShopRunState
relations: Stable for one run and refreshed only by allowed run-bound tokens.

domain_model.name: SaveSnapshot
relations: Atomically replaced and paired with rolling backups and a compact journal.

domain_model.name: JournalRecord
relations: Used only for important idempotent transitions such as banking, rewards, routes, completion, and suspension.

domain_model.name: DifficultyProfile
relations: Fixed at attempt start and remains deterministic for the run.

domain_model.name: BuildIdentity
relations: Included in diagnostics and immutable test artifacts.

frontend_screens.name: Menu Hub
frontend_screens.notes: Fast menu-driven entry to mission selection, armory, records, settings, and reward inbox.

frontend_screens.name: Mission Select and Difficulty
frontend_screens.notes: Shows fixed-per-attempt rules, expected recovery, and evidence-affecting conditions.

frontend_screens.name: Loadout and Armory
frontend_screens.notes: Supports four slots, comparison, checkpoint/shop changes, and clear weapon-state explanations.

frontend_screens.name: In-Mission HUD
frontend_screens.notes: Prioritizes four independent weapon states, power banks, thruster recharge, secured versus at-risk loot, and readable warnings.

frontend_screens.name: Fog-of-War Map
frontend_screens.notes: Reveals explored rooms and discovered services without exposing hidden secrets.

frontend_screens.name: Teleport and Shop
frontend_screens.notes: Safe non-combat travel and one selected shop-enabled teleport in the factory slice.

frontend_screens.name: Secure Storage
frontend_screens.notes: Explicitly previews and atomically confirms banking.

frontend_screens.name: Mission Results and Records
frontend_screens.notes: Supports immediate replay and distinguishes technically invalid or command-altered sessions.

frontend_screens.name: Strongbox Vault and Reward Review
frontend_screens.notes: Collection-ordered, atomic, capacity-safe, reload-proof reveal and review.

frontend_screens.name: Settings and Accessibility
frontend_screens.notes: Scalable UI, reduced effects, camera feedback, aim assistance, hold/toggle, audio, display, and rebinding.

frontend_screens.name: Diagnostics Export
frontend_screens.notes: Creates a privacy-safe support bundle only on explicit user action.

backend_services.name: No Remote MVP Backend
backend_services.responsibility: The internal MVP has no mandatory server, account, analytics, cloud-save, matchmaking, or storefront service.

backend_services.name: Local Save and Recovery Service
backend_services.responsibility: Validates, snapshots, journals, backs up, migrates, exports, imports, and safely recovers local profile and mission data.

backend_services.name: Local Content Registry Service
backend_services.responsibility: Resolves stable IDs to typed content definitions and rejects duplicates, missing references, unsupported versions, and generated-registry drift.

backend_services.name: Local Diagnostics Service
backend_services.responsibility: Records bounded structured events, rotates local logs, marks invalid evidence, and exports explicit privacy-safe support bundles.

backend_services.name: Local Artifact Identity Service
backend_services.responsibility: Exposes source, editor, dependency, content, test, symbol, checksum, and save-schema identity for immutable formal builds.

core_engine_responsibilities: Run deterministic engine-independent movement, weapon, mission, reward, progression, and persistence rules where practical.
Keep one authoritative MissionRunState and reject direct durable-state mutation from Unity scenes.
Translate device-independent Input System actions into movement, shared aiming, four-weapon fire, power mode, thruster, interaction, map, and menu intents.
Integrate Unity 2D physics for precise planar movement, collision, projectiles, wall ricochet, shove behavior, and deterministic safeguards.
Run four mounted weapon state machines independently under one fire command and one aim point.
Project authoritative mission and room state into additive scenes and modular content packages.
Coordinate explicit bootstrap, services, registries, commands, events, and scene lifecycle without scene searches or lifecycle luck.
Render dimensional 2D sprites through URP 2D while preserving readability without dynamic lighting.
Enforce stable IDs, typed definitions, generated registries, deterministic review snapshots, and content-package validation.
Persist atomic snapshots and a bounded idempotent journal with migration and recovery.
Expose capability-gated diagnostics, deterministic setup, fault injection, performance overlays, and evidence classification.
Build reproducible Windows artifacts and measure CPU, GPU, allocations, memory, loading, projectile, enemy, light, particle, atlas, and audio budgets.
verification_tasks: Validate every generated planning JSON artifact against its framework contract and ensure no task backlog or task batch is created.
Compile the pinned Unity project and run deterministic edit-mode tests for domain rules and content validation.
Run focused play-mode tests for input, movement, collision, four-weapon operation, room projection, save/resume, banking, rewards, and clean scene transitions.
Run deterministic replay tests for RewardCommitment, atomic single granting, shop stock, mission-only refresh tokens, anti-farming, and interrupted transactions.
Run save corruption, interrupted write, journal replay, backup fallback, migration, newer-schema refusal, import, export, and suspend-consumption tests.
Run benchmark and short-route sessions with representative effect density, local diagnostics, quick restart, settings persistence, and near-target frame rate.
Run Windows player smoke tests for launch, menu, input, arena/room load, combat, checkpoint, save, resume, completion, replay, and clean exit.
Run accessibility checks for scalable UI, color-independent telegraphs, reduced effects, configurable shake, aim assistance, hold/toggle, audio/display controls, and rebinding.
Run representative heavy-combat stress scenes and real-hardware checks against the primary 1080p/60 target and the reduced-quality minimum floor.
Run immutable artifact checks for source commit, Unity version, package lock, generated-content fingerprints, symbols, checksums, test reports, and save compatibility.
Conduct human playable review for movement feel, boost chaining, ricochet predictability, four-weapon readability, enemy pressure, route pacing, audio impact, UI clarity, and replay desire.
Conduct an independent content-pipeline reproduction in an isolated branch and verify that a weapon, enemy, or room addition does not require unrelated foundational edits.

starter_prompts.intake_interviewer: Use the merged Product Discovery record only for historical traceability; do not reopen discovery unless the human explicitly changes product requirements.
starter_prompts.planning_agent: Maintain the planning package and planning-run trace only; preserve merged requirements, keep executable task decomposition deferred, and update handoff state through the planning PR.
starter_prompts.contract_steward: Own stable IDs, domain/application boundaries, schemas, generated registries, save contracts, command/event contracts, and cross-lane interface review before implementation lanes consume them.
starter_prompts.frontend_builder: Own menu hub, HUD, map, loadout, rewards, settings, accessibility, and diagnostics presentation; consume application contracts and never own durable mission truth.
starter_prompts.backend_builder: There is no remote MVP backend. Own only local save/recovery, content registry, diagnostics export, and artifact identity services behind project-owned interfaces.
starter_prompts.core_engine_builder: Own engine-independent gameplay/state rules and Unity adapters for input, 2D physics, scenes, rendering, audio, and build integration while preserving one authoritative domain state.
starter_prompts.red_team_verifier: Attack determinism, duplicate grants, rollback exploits, shop refresh abuse, save corruption, scene-state divergence, input edge cases, performance overload, accessibility regressions, and planning-scope drift.
